posts.text: Two oil tankers and two LPG carriers are crossing the Strait of Hormuz **inbound** via the Omani route with their AIS turned on (under heavy US Navy / Airforce watch). Washington trying hard to keep the Omani route on.
posts.summary:
# Oil Market Summary

The U.S. is actively monitoring and supporting shipping traffic through the Strait of Hormuz via the Omani route to maintain a critical oil and gas transit corridor. This effort suggests Washington is working to prevent supply disruptions through one of the world's most strategically important chokepoints for global energy markets.

posts.text: Ok I have a cool hack for making your 4th of July burgers incredibly juicy. Mix 1/4 tsp. of baking soda with 1 TBS. of water. Make a slurry and mix per pound of ground beef. Thank me later.
posts.summary: This post contains a recipe tip for cooking burgers and has no implications for the oil market.

posts.text:
Iran’s foreign affairs minister:

“… Any attempt to adopt new or separate arrangements compared to what is underway by the Islamic Republic of Iran, will only lead to more complicated situations and delays in the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, and will increase the tensions,
posts.summary: Iran's foreign minister is warning that alternative arrangements to its current proposals regarding the Strait of Hormuz could escalate tensions and delay reopening, potentially creating supply risks for oil markets dependent on this critical chokepoint. The statement suggests Iran may resist multilateral or competing proposals, signaling hardened negotiating positions around one of the world's most important oil transit routes.

posts.text: Spanish import hub urges EU to delay ban on Russian gas https://t.co/4bbL2FMJzu

posts.text:
Despite the destabilising effect of the Middle East conflict, global energy investment is set to grow to $3.4 trillion in 2026.

Advanced economies and China are expected to make up over 70% of this spending.

The sustained investment growth suggests continued development across energy sectors despite regional geopolitical risks.
